explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.findings: An audit report documents an agreement to enhance board-level disclosure of director skills, experience, attributes, and demographic information. This report also states responsiveness to shareholder proposals on transparency, supporting external accountability.
external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Publish third-party audit results, certifications, or regulatory compliance documentation.

relevant_sources.summary: This audit report from the NYC Retirement Systems provides evidence for **external accountability, fairness, governance, and oversight**. The report documents NextEra Energy's agreement to enhance board-level disclosure of director skills, experience, attributes, and demographic information, demonstrating responsiveness to shareholder proposals on transparency and supporting external accountability and governance. Furthermore, the mention of a "Civil Rights Audit" and "algorithmic bias" within the context of board oversight of high-impact algorithmic risks indicates a commitment to fairness and oversight in AI governance.
relevant_sources.title: NYC Retirement Systems 2025 Shareholder Initiatives Report

fairness.findings: An audit report references a "Civil Rights Audit" and "algorithmic bias" within the context of board oversight of high-impact algorithmic risks. This indicates a focus on fairness considerations in AI governance.
fairness.path_to_improvement: Publish bias testing results, outcome monitoring, or vendor fairness certifications.

governance.findings: Documentation describes automated decision-making processes powered by AI/ML, and an annual report mentions AI risks to infrastructure. A technical paper details a 4-tier governance framework, including policy development for approved AI usage and the implementation of controls and guardrails, also referencing "Responsible AI services" and "AI governance and access controls." An audit report documents an agreement to enhance board-level disclosure of director information and references "Civil Rights Audit" and "algorithmic bias" within board oversight of high-impact algorithmic risks.

relevant_sources.summary: This NextEra 360 brochure, a technical overview, provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. The document's description of "AUTOMATIC DISPATCH" and the ability to "predict and optimize asset performance" suggests automated decision-making processes powered by AI/ML, which are key aspects of governance. Furthermore, the explicit mention of these AI/ML capabilities supports transparency by indicating how the platform utilizes these technologies.
relevant_sources.title: NextEra 360 Brochure - Technical Overview

relevant_sources.summary: This company-owned blog post provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. It supports governance by mentioning a specific manager role and the system's impact, implying oversight and accountability. The blog post enhances transparency by detailing the AI system's deployment, scope, capabilities, and what it measures, even using an analogy to explain its predictive function.
relevant_sources.title: Changing The Current: Algorithms are Saving FPL Millions of Dollars

relevant_sources.summary: The NextEra Energy 2024 Form 10-K Annual Report provides evidence for the **governance** pillar of responsible AI. The report mentions AI risks to infrastructure, which implies a need for governance, although it does not describe specific execution or formal commitments related to AI governance.
relevant_sources.title: NextEra Energy 2024 Form 10-K Annual Report

relevant_sources.summary: This technical paper from AWS re:Invent 2024 provides evidence for the governance, privacy, and transparency pillars of responsible AI. It details NextEra Energy's 4-tier governance framework, which includes policy development for approved AI usage and the implementation of controls and guardrails, supporting the governance pillar. The paper also mentions alignment assessments and the use of "Responsible AI services" and "AI governance and access controls," indicating a commitment to transparency and privacy in AI implementation.
relevant_sources.title: AWS re:Invent 2024 - NextEra Energy's Gen AI Journey

relevant_sources.summary: This blog post provides evidence for the **governance** pillar of responsible AI. It discusses NextEra's existing board matrix disclosure, which includes skills, attributes, and gender, and notes shareholder support for more detailed disclosure. While the source mentions "AI-powered boardroom" and "governance blueprint," these references suggest a policy or framework for AI in governance rather than specific operational details.
relevant_sources.title: Board Matrix Disclosure - Governance Intelligence Analysis

oversight.findings: An audit report references board oversight of high-impact algorithmic risks, indicating a commitment to oversight in AI governance.
oversight.path_to_improvement: Publish override mechanisms, escalation processes, or appeal workflows.

privacy.findings: A corporate privacy policy describes the company's commitment to protecting personal information and outlines opt-out mechanisms for data processing related to personalized advertising and optimization. A technical paper also mentions alignment assessments for AI implementation and references the use of "Responsible AI services" and "AI governance and access controls."
privacy.path_to_improvement: Publish AI-specific privacy impact assessments or data handling procedures.

relevant_sources.summary: This corporate privacy policy document provides evidence for the **privacy** pillar. It supports this pillar by describing the company's commitment to protecting personal information, including data collected for personalized advertising and optimization through third-party tools like Google Analytics and Ads, and by outlining opt-out mechanisms for such processing.
relevant_sources.title: Privacy Policy - NextEra Energy Corporate

transparency.findings: Documentation describes automated decision-making processes powered by AI/ML and specifies how the platform utilizes these capabilities. A blog post details the AI system's deployment, scope, capabilities, and what it measures, using an analogy to explain its predictive function. Technical papers mention alignment assessments and reference the use of "Responsible AI services" and "AI governance and access controls," while other documents highlight AI's success in minimizing fossil fuel use and describe AI making recommendations.

relevant_sources.summary: The "NextEra Energy Resources - AI Infrastructure" document provides evidence for the **transparency** pillar of responsible AI. It highlights AI's success in minimizing fossil fuel use and identifying opportunities, implying transparency of its capabilities. Additionally, the document describes AI making recommendations, suggesting transparency in its function, though it lacks specific operational or policy details for classification.
relevant_sources.title: NextEra Energy Resources - AI Infrastructure

summary.overall_findings: NextEra Energy's public disclosures address transparency and governance at the operational level, with materials describing automated decision-making processes powered by AI/ML. Overall, 6 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars have documented evidence, with fairness, oversight, privacy, and external accountability addressed through corporate policies. An audit report references a "Civil Rights Audit" in the context of board oversight of high-impact algorithmic risks, while a corporate privacy policy outlines opt-out mechanisms for processing data collected for personalized advertising and optimization. No qualifying public evidence was found for explainability, and the assessment draws on 23 publicly available sources.
